page: Amazon Web Services r8idb.metal-48xl is a bare-metal, memory-optimized server instance featuring Intel processors. It is configured with 192 virtual CPUs across 96 physical cores, utilizing an x86_64 architecture with dedicated CPU allocation. The processors operate at a clock speed of 3.9 GHz. The system is equipped with 1536.0 GB of system memory, providing a density of 8.0 GB of RAM per virtual core.

This instance includes integrated local instance store volumes, providing 11400 GB of local NVMe SSD storage. It features a baseline network bandwidth of 200 Gbps. There are no complimentary public IPv4 addresses included with this configuration, and it does not contain any graphics processing units.

From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the bare-metal architecture and high density of both memory and local NVMe storage position this server for resource-intensive enterprise workloads. The combination of 1.5 TB of RAM and over 11 TB of local high-speed storage makes it suitable for large-scale in-memory databases, big data analytics, and memory-bound enterprise applications that require direct hardware access and high network throughput.
description: Amazon Web Services r8idb.metal-48xl is a bare-metal, memory-optimized server powered by Intel x86_64 processors. It features 192 dedicated vCPUs (96 physical cores) operating at 3.9 GHz, paired with 1536.0 GB of system memory, yielding 8.0 GB of RAM per virtual core. The instance is bundled with 11400 GB of local NVMe SSD storage and provides a baseline network bandwidth of 200 Gbps. It does not include any GPUs or complimentary public IPv4 addresses. This bare-metal configuration is designed for workloads requiring direct hardware access, substantial memory capacity, and high-speed local storage, such as large-scale in-memory databases, data warehousing, and intensive enterprise applications.
